title: rubus inclinabilis
intro: Rubus inclinabilis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or the creeping raspberry, is a perennial shrub native to the Himalayan region. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and stems, which contain anthocyanins and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus inclinabilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain diseases such as cancer and diabetes. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and can also be used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
